hero.title: Épicurien French Bakery
hero.subtitle: Award-winning Paris croissant. Now in Bangkok.

story_chef.title: The Chef
story_chef.body: Trained at École Ferrandi Paris, Chef Enzo Le Bohec earned the title of Best Croissant in Île-de-France in 2021. He brought his craft — and his sourdough starter — to Bangkok, where he founded Épicurien French Bakery.
story_chef.quote: I won. It took courage, precision, and a love for the craft that borders on obsession.

pull_quote.text: Je souhaite plus tard ouvrir une boulangerie en Thaïlande.

story_geste.title: The Craft
story_geste.body: 27 layers of pure butter dough, folded by hand. Vanilla sourced from Madagascar. A crust that shatters on touch and melts in memory. Every croissant is a meditation on technique — baked fresh each morning before dawn.

menu_page.title: Menu
menu_page.subtitle: Fresh pastries and breads, baked daily from premium ingredients.

visit_page.title: Find Us
visit_page.subtitle: Come for the croissant. Stay for the ambiance.

ambiance.title: Warm Ambiance
ambiance.body: A cozy, light-filled space inspired by Parisian boulangeries.

quality.title: Exceptional Quality
quality.body: Award-winning recipes, locally sourced ingredients, made fresh daily.

location.title: Prime Location

awards.title: Awards & Distinctions
awards.award_description: Organised at Lycée d'Étiolles, Essonne. Chef Enzo Le Bohec — then 17 years old and training at École Ferrandi Paris — won among 9 candidates from Paris, Essonne, and Yvelines. Judged on form, colour, texture, and flavour by 5 professional jurors.

footer.tagline: Award-winning French pastry in the heart of Bangkok.
